title: pedicularis resupinata var. umbrosa
intro: Pedicularis resupinata var. umbrosa, also known as a type of lousewort, is a plant species within the bartsia family (Orobanchaceae) that is native to North America. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as menstrual cramps and other gynecological problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, relieve nausea, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Pedicularis resupinata var. umbrosa can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction for its medicinal properties.
